title: mauhlia umbellata
intro: Mauhlia umbellata, also known as Buttonbush, is a perennial shrub native to North America, characterized by its distinctive spherical flower clusters resembling buttons. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of mucilages and iridoid glycosides. The health benefits of Mauhlia umbellata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
